discussion.p: Hardening `view_interface::operator[]` to prevent out-of-bounds access is worthwhile for the `sized_range` cases, and we have already done so for other standard views such as `span` and `string_view`'s `operator[]`.

li.p: Modify , class template `view_interface` synopsis, as indicated:

blockquote.p:
-3- Hardened preconditions: `!empty()` is `true`.
-4- Effects: Equivalent to: return *ranges::prev(ranges::end(derived()));

p.ins: -?- Hardened preconditions: If `D` models `sized_range`, then n >= 0 && n < ranges::distance(derived()) is `true`.
